response.justification: Bogdan has strong technical depth across 15+ years and some relevant developer tooling exposure (CI/CD setup, test frameworks, MCP tooling), but the resume shows no formal people management experience with direct reports, which is a hard requirement for this mid-to-senior management role. His background is primarily that of a senior/staff individual contributor doing full-stack product work, and while he has mentored through workshops and katas, there is no evidence of owning team performance, conducting reviews, or driving organizational change at scale. The role's emphasis on build systems and mobile platform ecosystems at scale is also largely unaddressed in his history.
strengths: 15+ years of broad technical experience spanning web, mobile, embedded, and tooling gives credibility when guiding engineers across a diverse stack

Genuine developer-tooling contribution: built open-source test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), set up CI/GitLab infrastructure at Altom, and is currently working on MCP code generators and agentic developer tooling at re:cinq
Demonstrated knowledge-sharing culture: organized recurring Refactoring Dojos and Code Katas, delivered workshops and conference talks, and co-created a multi-module Selenium course
concerns: No evidence of people management: no mention of direct reports, performance reviews, headcount planning, or formal team leadership beyond a solo founder role on an open-source side project

Developer-tooling experience is shallow relative to the requirement—CI server maintenance and personal test libraries do not demonstrate ownership of build systems, automated release pipelines, or testing infrastructure at production scale
No indication of strategic roadmap ownership, cross-team alignment, or organizational change leadership, which are central to a Level II Engineering Manager role

key_factors.factor: Limited build/tooling systems depth at scale
Broad technical credibility and teaching track record
